d1.title: Strength A (Lower-body anchored)
d1.stimulus: Establish baseline RPE on primary squat or deadlift. Learn movement standards.

structure.description: Easy bike or row + dynamic mobility.
A1. Back Squat: 4×5 @ RPE 6-7 — find weight where rep 5 feels solid, 3-4 reps in reserve.
B1. Walking Lunges: 3×20 steps unweighted or light DB

notes.about: Foundation intro session. Calibrate RPE — pick a weight where rep 5 feels solid with 3-4 reps in reserve. Quick Rox preserves both A-pair lifts; time savings come from skipping accessories.
notes.week_context: Wk1 RPE 6-7 → Wk2 RPE 7 → Wk3 RPE 7 with set count up.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Goblet squat or bodyweight squat. DB RDL.

notes.substitutions: No barbell: DB front-rack squat, single-leg DB RDL.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible (useful Z2 self-check).
notes.week_context: Wk1 Easy Run is 35 min main work.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Run/walk intervals to keep effort easy.

d3.title: Strength B (Upper-body anchored)
d3.stimulus: Establish baseline on press/pull. Build pulling endurance for race-day grip demands.

structure.description: A1. Bench Press or Overhead Press: 4×5 @ RPE 6-7
A2. Bent-over Row or Pull-up: 4×8 @ RPE 6-7
B2. Farmers Carry: 3×40m at moderate load (~40-50% race weight)

notes.about: Press + pull baseline. Farmers carry at moderate load to start grip endurance.
notes.week_context: Wk1 RPE 6-7 — calibrate.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Ring rows, DB bench. Light farmer carries.

d4.title: Hyrox Movement Pattern
d4.stimulus: Learn all 8 stations at light load. Movement standards focus. Stations programmed at 25-60% of race volume/load, 1-2 sets each.

structure.description: Rotate through 8 stations, 1-2 sets each. Rest 60 sec between stations. Movement quality focus, not speed.
Same 8 stations at half the volume each.

notes.about: First exposure to all 8 race stations at sub-race volume and load. Focus is mechanics, not speed. Sandbag lunge load runs lighter than other stations per Framework Spec §9.
notes.week_context: Wk1 establishes 8-station vocabulary at 25-60% race volume/load. Wk2 ramps to 65-70% with race-weight sled focus block.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Stay at 25-50% loads. Skip Wall Balls if depth or target consistency are questionable.
scaling.advanced: Hold Foundation loads — race-weight exposure builds gradually.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ), ring rows (sled pull).

d5.title: Easy Run
d5.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Second Easy Run of the week. Same target as D2 — keep it conversational.
notes.week_context: Foundation builds aerobic base with two Easy Runs per week.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

notes.about: Foundation Wk1 — intro. 5 sessions establishing baselines on key lifts, two Easy Runs for aerobic base, one full 8-station rotation at light load. Calibrate RPE this week.
notes.week_context: Foundation Wk1 → Wk2 → Wk3 builds aerobic time from ~70 min to ~90 min/week and station load from 25-60% to ~75% race weight.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

weeks.title: Foundation — Wk2 (Development)
weeks.summary: Foundation development. RPE cap 7 (with brief race-weight sled focus block on D4).

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Add 5-10 lbs from Wk1 if Wk1 felt sub-RPE 7. Settle into RPE 7 working weights.

structure.description: B1. Walking Lunges: 3×20 steps with light DB
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge: 3×12 each side

notes.about: RPE 7 working weights. Add Single-leg Glute Bridge to the unilateral progression.
notes.week_context: Wk2 sits between Wk1 calibration and Wk3 set-count progression.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Stay at unweighted lunges. Goblet squat if barbell isn't dialed.

notes.substitutions: DB squat variants if no barbell.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Easy Run main work bumps to 40 min.
notes.week_context: Foundation aerobic time builds Wk1 35 → Wk2 40 → Wk3 45 min main.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Press/pull at RPE 7. Farmers carry distance increases slightly.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×60m at 50% race weight

notes.about: Settle into RPE 7. Farmers carry steps up to 60m to build grip endurance.
notes.week_context: Wk2 farmers 60m → Wk3 80m → Wk4 80m loaded heavier.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Ring rows, DB bench, lighter farmer load.

d4.title: Hyrox Movement Pattern
d4.stimulus: 8-station rotation at 65-70% race weight + brief race-weight sled focus block. Note on RPE: the race-weight sled focus block briefly exceeds the Wk2 RPE 7 cap. This is intentional — a controlled, technique-focused first exposure to race-weight handling. Total dose is 3×12.5m at full race weight with ample rest, which is below race-station distance and below race-fatigue context.

structure.description: 8-station rotation (same as Wk1 D4) at 65-70% race weight where applicable.
details: Sandbag Lunges 50m at 50% race weight (per Framework Spec §9)

structure.description: Race-weight sled focus block: 3 rounds of 12.5m sled push at race weight, 60-90 sec rest between rounds. First race-weight sled exposure — focus on body position, not speed.
4 stations at 65-70% race weight (matches Full Rox baseline rotation, does NOT include the race-weight sled focus block). These four cover the highest-skill stations to learn first.

notes.about: Second Movement Pattern session ramps load to 65-70% race weight and adds first race-weight sled exposure as a brief focus block.
notes.week_context: Wk2 D4 first race-weight sled handling. Wk3 D4 expands sled push/pull to full 50m race distance at 75% load.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Skip race-weight focus block. Stay at 50-60% loads on rotation.
scaling.advanced: Sled focus block at race weight as written — control body position.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

d5.title: Engine
d5.stimulus: First aerobic-ceiling session in a non-running modality.

structure.description: 30 min steady-state on rower OR bike at Z2-low Z3 (RPE 6-7).

notes.about: Non-running aerobic capacity work. Pick rower or bike — whichever feels less like running.
notes.week_context: Engine appears in Foundation Wk2-3 and returns in Wk7 Mini-Deload.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.advanced: Hold RPE 7 — don't push to threshold.

notes.substitutions: Air bike or SkiErg if no rower/bike.

notes.about: Foundation Wk2 — development. RPE settles at 7 across strength. Movement Pattern adds first race-weight sled exposure as a controlled focus block. Engine debuts.
notes.week_context: Wk2 sits between Wk1 calibration and Wk3 Foundation peak.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

weeks.title: Foundation — Wk3 (Peak)
weeks.summary: Foundation peak. RPE cap 7. Set count up; full 8-station race-distance rotation.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Set count up to 5×5 squats. RPE holds at 7.

structure.description: B1. Walking Lunges: 3×24 steps with DB
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge: 3×12 each side
B3. Side Plank: 3×40 sec each side

notes.about: Foundation peak: more sets, same RPE. Total volume up ~25% from Wk1.
notes.week_context: Wk3 caps Foundation strength volume before Build introduces RPE 8.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.advanced: Stay RPE 7 — Build introduces RPE 8 next week.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Foundation Easy Run peak — 45 min main work.
notes.week_context: Foundation aerobic peak before Build adds Recovery + Tempo + Compromised.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Set count up to 5×5 press, 5×6 row/pull. RPE holds at 7.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×80m at 60% race weight

notes.about: Foundation strength peak. Farmers up to 80m.
notes.week_context: Wk3 caps Foundation strength volume.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

d4.title: Hyrox Movement Pattern
d4.stimulus: Full 8-station rotation, one exposure per station. Most stations at partial race volume; sled push/pull at full race distance with reduced load (turf-marking precludes clean partial sled distance below 25m).

structure.description: Full 8-station rotation. Rest 60-90 sec between stations.
details: Sandbag Lunges 75m at 65% race weight (sandbag lunge load is intentionally lower than other stations; see Framework Spec §9)
Wall Balls 40 reps at race weight (smooth sets only)

structure.description: 4 stations at 50% of corresponding Full Rox prescription. These four complete the 8-station Foundation exposure rotation when paired with Wk2 D4 Quick Rox.

notes.about: Foundation movement-pattern peak. Sled push/pull move to full race distance (with reduced load) since 12.5m turf marking precludes clean partial sled distance below 25m. Wall balls at race weight, smooth sets only.
notes.week_context: Wk3 D4 caps the Movement Pattern progression before Build switches to Compromised Running and Tempo.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Hold loads at 50-60%; reduce wall ball reps to 25.
scaling.advanced: Hold per spec — Build introduces race-weight Compromised next week.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

d5.title: Engine
d5.stimulus: Engine peak. Steady state with mid-session Z3 efforts.

structure.description: 35 min steady-state at Z2-Z3, plus 4×2 min Z3 efforts in middle with 1 min easy between.
22 min steady-state with 3×1 min Z3 efforts.

notes.about: Foundation Engine peak. First Z3 efforts in the cycle.
notes.week_context: Engine appears Foundation + Peak only. Returns in Wk7.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

scaling.beginner: Drop Z3 efforts; hold steady state.
scaling.advanced: Hold RPE 7 ceiling on Z3 efforts.

notes.about: Foundation Wk3 — peak. Strength set counts up. Movement Pattern caps with full 8-station rotation. Engine adds Z3 efforts.
notes.week_context: Wk3 caps the Foundation block. Build introduces Tempo + Recovery Run + Compromised Running.
notes.block_context: Foundation block (Wk 1-3): establish aerobic base, learn station mechanics at light load, build movement quality. RPE caps at 7.

weeks.title: Build — Wk4 (Intro)
weeks.summary: Build intro. Tempo, Recovery, Compromised introduced. RPE cap 8.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Build introduces RPE 7-8 on the squat. Hip Thrust replaces glute bridge for heavier posterior loading.

notes.about: First RPE 8 squat exposure. Add hip thrust for heavier glute work.
notes.week_context: Wk4 RPE 7-8 → Wk5 RPE 8 → Wk6 RPE 8 with reps down to 5×3.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.
notes.substitutions: DB hip thrust if no barbell.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: 45 min Z2 — Build holds Foundation peak distance.
notes.week_context: Easy Run main work holds 45 min Wk4-6.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Press + pull push to RPE 7-8. Farmers loaded to 70% race weight.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×80m at 70% race weight

notes.about: Build intro upper. RPE 7-8.
notes.week_context: Build strength ramps RPE 7-8 → 8 → 8 across Wk4-6.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

scaling.advanced: Push to RPE 8 if grip and form hold.

d4.title: Tempo Run
d4.stimulus: First lactate threshold work. Z3 sustained intervals.

structure.description: 4×5 min Z3 (RPE 7-8) with 90 sec easy jog between. 20 min total work.
3×4 min Z3 with 90 sec easy. 12 min total.

notes.about: Tempo intro: 4×5 min Z3 with short rest. First threshold work in the cycle.
notes.week_context: Wk4 4×5 min → Wk5 5×5 min → Wk6 4×6 min.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

scaling.beginner: 3×5 min Z3 if 4 rounds blow up.
scaling.advanced: Hold Z3 — don't drift to Z4.

notes.about: Recovery Run = active flush. Deliberately slower than Easy Run.
notes.week_context: Recovery Run holds 30 min Wk4-9. Trims to 25 min in Wk10.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d6.title: Compromised Running
d6.stimulus: Most race-specific session type. Run + station under fatigue. First Compromised session uses race weight on all stations, with sled push distance reduced (25m vs 50m race distance) and sandbag lunge load slightly reduced (75-85% race weight vs full race weight) to ease into the new session type.

structure.description: 4 rounds of (1 km run at Z3 + 1 station). 90 sec rest between rounds.
details: Round 1: 1km + 25 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 1km + 25m sled push at race weight (intentionally short — first Compromised sled exposure, 2 turf lengths)
Round 3: 1km + 50m sandbag lunges at 75-85% race weight (load reduced for first Compromised lunge exposure under fatigue)
Round 4: 1km + 25 wall balls at race weight
Round 1: 600m + 20 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 600m + 12.5m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 600m + 37.5m sandbag lunges at 75% race weight

notes.about: First Compromised session. Eases in: sled push at 25m (half race distance), sandbag at 75-85% race weight. Wall balls at race weight in R1 + R4 bookend.
notes.week_context: Wk5 D6 jumps to 5 rounds at full race weight + distance. Wk6 D6 is Half-Sim format (race-order stations 1-4).
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

scaling.beginner: 3 rounds. Drop sled push distance to 12.5m.
scaling.advanced: Hold prescription — Wk5 D6 ramps to full race distance.

notes.about: Build Wk4 — intro. Tempo, Recovery Run, and Compromised Running added. Strength pushes to RPE 7-8. First Compromised session eases in with reduced sled distance and sandbag load.
notes.week_context: Wk4 introduces three new session types. Wk5 progresses each; Wk6 caps with first Half-Sim.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

weeks.title: Build — Wk5 (Progression)
weeks.summary: Build progression. RPE pushes to 8. Compromised at full race weight + distance.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Squat at RPE 8. Walking lunges add a set.

notes.about: RPE 8 on squat — settle in. Walking lunge volume up to 4 sets.
notes.week_context: Wk5 holds 5×5 RPE 8 squat. Wk6 drops reps to 5×3 at higher load.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.
notes.substitutions: DB hip thrust if no barbell.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: 45 min Z2 — Build holds.
notes.week_context: Easy Run main work holds 45 min through Build.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Press/pull at RPE 8. Farmers up to 100m.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×100m at 75% race weight

notes.about: RPE 8 settled. Farmers 3×100m at 75% race weight.
notes.week_context: Build upper progression Wk4-6.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

structure.description: 5×5 min Z3 with 90 sec easy. 25 min total.
4×4 min Z3 with 90 sec easy. 16 min total.

notes.about: Tempo volume bumps from 4×5 to 5×5.
notes.week_context: Wk5 5×5 → Wk6 4×6 (longer reps, fewer rounds).
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

scaling.advanced: Hold Z3 — don't drift to Z4.

notes.about: Recovery Run holds 30 min main.
notes.week_context: Holds Wk4-9 then trims in Wk10.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d6.title: Compromised Running
d6.stimulus: First Compromised session at race weight + full race distance across all 5 rounds. Five different stations rotated through, including SkiErg as a Compromised exposure (closing the SkiErg-under-fatigue practice gap).

structure.description: 5 rounds of (1 km run at Z3 + 1 station at race weight). 90 sec rest between rounds.
details: Round 1: 1km + 25 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 1km + 50m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 1km + 50m sandbag lunges at race weight
Round 4: 1km + 50m sled pull at race weight
Round 1: 600m + 20 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 600m + 25m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 600m + 25m sandbag lunges at race weight

notes.about: First Compromised at full race weight + full race distance. Adds SkiErg under fatigue (closing v3.1 coverage gap).
notes.week_context: Wk6 D6 switches to Half-Sim format (race-order stations 1-4).
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

notes.about: Build Wk5 — progression. Strength settles at RPE 8. Compromised at full race weight + distance with SkiErg added.
notes.week_context: Wk5 sits between Build intro (Wk4) and Build peak (Wk6).
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

weeks.title: Build — Wk6 (Peak — Half-Sim Stations 1-4)
weeks.summary: Build peak. First Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE cap 8.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Heavier load, lower reps. Squat 5×3 @ RPE 8.

structure.description: A1. Back Squat: 5×3 @ RPE 8 (heavier load, lower reps)

notes.about: Build peak — heavier load, lower reps on the squat. RPE 8 maintained.
notes.week_context: Wk6 caps Build strength. Wk7 deload drops load 15-20%.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Build peak Easy Run — 45 min Z2 holds.
notes.week_context: Easy Run holds 45 min through Build.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Heavier press, lower reps. Farmers up to 80% race weight.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×100m at 80% race weight

notes.about: Build upper peak. Heavier press load.
notes.week_context: Wk6 caps Build upper. Wk7 deloads.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d4.title: Tempo Run
d4.stimulus: Longer reps, fewer rounds: 4×6 min Z3.

structure.description: 4×6 min Z3 with 90 sec easy. 24 min total.
3×5 min Z3 with 90 sec easy. 15 min total.

notes.about: Longer Z3 reps to push lactate clearance.
notes.week_context: Wk6 caps Build tempo. Wk7 drops Tempo entirely.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

notes.about: Recovery Run before Half-Sim.
notes.week_context: 30 min Z1-low Z2 holds.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

d6.title: Priority Station Half-Sim (race-order stations 1-4)
d6.stimulus: First race rehearsal. Race-order stations 1-4 (SkiErg → Sled Push → Sled Pull → Burpee Broad Jumps) at full race weight and full station volume. Pacing and transition practice. Full Rox is the strongly recommended option for this session.

structure.description: Half-sim at race weight, controlled effort (RPE 7-8, NOT race effort). Smooth transitions, sustainable pace, no all-out efforts.
details: 1 km run + Sled Push 50m at race weight
1 km run + Sled Pull 50m at race weight

structure.description: 3 stations + 3 runs at 65-75% race weight. Quick Rox is a fallback only — Full Rox is strongly recommended for this race-rehearsal session.
details: 600m run + Sled Push 25m at 75% race weight

notes.about: First race rehearsal. Race-order stations 1-4 at full race weight and full station volume. Pacing + transition practice. Full Rox strongly recommended.
notes.week_context: Wk8 D6 covers race-order stations 5-8 (Row, Farmers, Sandbag, Wall Balls). Wk10 D5 is Full Sim (all 8 stations).
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

scaling.beginner: Quick Rox version is the safe fallback; reduce sled load further if needed.
scaling.advanced: Hold race weight + race volume; resist racing the clock.

notes.substitutions: If sled access is limited, sub KB swings 25 reps for the sled push round (note: this changes the race-rehearsal value substantially).

notes.about: Build Wk6 — peak. First Half-Sim (race-order stations 1-4). Strength reps drop to 5×3 at RPE 8.
notes.week_context: Wk6 caps Build. Wk7 Mini-Deload follows.
notes.block_context: Build block (Wk 4-6): add intensity and race-specificity. Recovery Run, Tempo, Compromised Running introduced. Wk6 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 1-4. RPE caps at 8.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk7 (Mini-Deload)
weeks.summary: Mini-Deload. Volume cut ~25%, intensity ~20%. RPE cap 6. Compromised + Tempo dropped.

d1.title: Strength A (light)
d1.stimulus: Deload — reduce load 15-20% from Wk6. RPE 6.

structure.description: A1. Back Squat: 4×5 @ RPE 6 (deload — reduce load 15-20% from Wk6)
A1. Back Squat: 4×5 @ RPE 6 at lighter load

notes.about: Deload squat. Reduce load 15-20% from Wk6 — supercompensation week.
notes.week_context: Wk7 deload allows Wk8-9-10 peak stress.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.advanced: Hold the cut. Don't undo the deload.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Easy Run trims to 30 min for the deload week.
notes.week_context: Wk7 30 min → Wk8 47 min → Wk9 50 min (peak).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 2×60m at 50% race weight

notes.about: Deload upper. RPE 6 — pull back from Wk6 RPE 8.
notes.week_context: Wk7 deload before Wk8-9-10 peak.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d4.title: Technique EMOM
d4.stimulus: Movement quality focus. Low load. Active recovery with skill maintenance. Stations selected = highest-technique-density: wall balls (depth + clean catch), sled push (body angle), sandbag lunges (knee-to-floor depth), burpee broad jumps (landing mechanics). SkiErg, Row, Farmers Carry, and Sled Pull excluded as machine-pacing or grind-focused stations with less technique upside in this format.

structure.description: 32 min EMOM rotation (8 full rounds), alternating four technique-density stations.
details: Min 1: 8 wall balls (light, focus on depth and clean catch)
Min 2: 10m sled push (very light, body position focus)

structure.description: 16 min EMOM (4 full rounds) with same rotation at lighter loads, same rep counts as Full Rox (8 wall balls, 10m sled push, 10 sandbag lunges, 6 burpee broad jumps).

notes.about: Technique EMOM uses the four highest-technique-density stations: wall balls, sled push, sandbag lunges, BBJ. Skill maintenance during deload.
notes.week_context: Wk7 only. Replaces Compromised Running for the deload week.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Lighter loads; cut sled distance to 5m.
scaling.advanced: Hold light loads — this is a skill session, not strength.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

d5.title: Engine (moderate)
d5.stimulus: Engine returns at moderate intensity for the deload week.

structure.description: 30 min Z2-low Z3 on rower or bike.

notes.about: Moderate Engine for active recovery without taxing recovery further.
notes.week_context: Engine appears Foundation + Wk7 only in v3.2.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

notes.about: Peak Wk7 — Mini-Deload. Volume cut ~25%, intensity ~20%. Compromised + Tempo dropped this week. Engine + Technique EMOM in their place. Allows supercompensation before Wk8-9-10 peak intensity.
notes.week_context: Wk7 deload bridges Build peak (Wk6) and Peak entry (Wk8).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk8 (Half-Sim Stations 5-8)
weeks.summary: Peak entry. Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8 (Row, Farmers, Sandbag, Wall Balls). RPE cap 8.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Post-deload: full session count, ramp intensity. Squat back to 5×5 @ RPE 8.

notes.about: Post-deload return to RPE 8.
notes.week_context: Wk8 ramps; Wk9 is APEX (RPE 8-9, 5×3 squat).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.
notes.substitutions: DB hip thrust if no barbell.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Easy Run rebuilds to 47 min main work.
notes.week_context: Wk8 47 min → Wk9 50 min (peak).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Press at RPE 8. Farmers up to 120m at 80% race weight.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×120m at 80% race weight

notes.about: RPE 8 upper. Farmers volume builds toward race-weight Wk9.
notes.week_context: Wk8 80% farmers → Wk9 race-weight farmers.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d4.title: Tempo Run
d4.stimulus: Tempo returns post-deload at Z3-Z4. 5×5 min.

structure.description: 5×5 min Z3-Z4 with 90 sec easy. 25 min total.
4×4 min Z3-Z4 with 90 sec easy. 16 min total.

notes.about: Tempo returns post-deload. Z3-Z4 — slightly higher intensity than Wk5.
notes.week_context: Wk8 5×5 → Wk9 6×5 Z4 (apex).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

notes.about: Recovery Run before Half-Sim.
notes.week_context: 30 min Z1-low Z2 holds.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d6.title: Priority Station Half-Sim (race-order stations 5-8)
d6.stimulus: Half-sim at race weight, race-order stations 5-8 (Row → Farmers → Sandbag → Wall Balls). Wall balls in position 8 with race-fatigue context — first time athletes face the final station under accumulated Compromised fatigue. Full Rox is strongly recommended.

structure.description: 4 stations + 4 runs at race weight, controlled RPE 8. Estimated duration ~55-70 min depending on athlete pace.
details: 1 km run + Farmers Carry 200m at race weight
1 km run + Sandbag Lunges 100m at race weight
1 km run + Wall Balls 100 reps at race weight

structure.description: 3 stations + 3 runs at 75-80% race weight. Quick Rox is a fallback only — Full Rox is strongly recommended.
details: 600m run + Farmers Carry 100m at 75% race weight

notes.about: Second Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8. Wall balls land in position 8 with race-fatigue context — first race-order exposure to the final station after accumulated Compromised fatigue. Full Rox strongly recommended.
notes.week_context: Wk6 covered stations 1-4; Wk8 covers stations 5-8; Wk10 D5 is Full Sim (all 8).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Quick Rox is the safe fallback; reduce wall ball reps further if needed.
scaling.advanced: Hold full station volume on each — this is the most race-specific Half-Sim of the cycle.

notes.substitutions: DB farmer carry if no farmer handles. KB swings if no sandbag (note: changes lunge stimulus).

notes.about: Peak Wk8 — Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8 (Row, Farmers, Sandbag, Wall Balls). Wall balls in position 8 give first race-fatigue exposure to the final station.
notes.week_context: Wk8 entry to Peak. Wk9 is APEX (RPE 8-9). Wk10 is Full Sim.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk9 (APEX)
weeks.summary: APEX — hardest week of the cycle. RPE pushes to 8-9. Full Rox strongly recommended for all sessions.

d1.title: Strength A
d1.stimulus: Heaviest squat session of the cycle. Walking lunge accessory volume is moderately reduced (3×20 from earlier 4×30) to manage local quad fatigue accumulation across Wk8 D6 → Wk9 D1.

structure.description: A1. Back Squat: 4×3 @ RPE 9 (heaviest squat session of the cycle, 1 rep in reserve)
B1. Walking Lunges: 3×20 steps loaded (reduced from 4×30 to manage Wk8 D6 → Wk9 D1 lunge fatigue layering)
A1. Back Squat: 4×3 @ RPE 9 at peak load

notes.about: APEX squat — heaviest session of the cycle. Walking lunge accessory deliberately reduced to protect Wk8 D6 → Wk9 D1 lunge fatigue layering.
notes.week_context: Wk9 caps strength stimulus. Wk10 reduces strength to allow Full Sim recovery.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Stay RPE 8 — leave 2 reps in reserve.
scaling.advanced: Hold RPE 9 — 1 rep in reserve, no misses.

notes.substitutions: DB hip thrust if no barbell.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible. Volume peak.

notes.about: Easy Run peak — 50 min. Apex Z2 volume.
notes.week_context: Wk9 50 min is the cycle peak. Wk10 trims to 40 min.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Run/walk. Drop to 40 min if cumulative fatigue is high.

d3.title: Strength B
d3.stimulus: Press at RPE 9. Farmers at race weight.

structure.description: B2. Farmers Carry: 3×120m at race weight

notes.about: APEX upper. Farmers at race weight for the first time.
notes.week_context: Wk9 caps upper. Wk11 holds race-weight farmers at reduced volume (2×80m).
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Stay RPE 8 — leave 2 reps in reserve.

d4.title: Tempo Run (apex)
d4.stimulus: Highest tempo volume of the cycle. 30 min total threshold work.

structure.description: 6×5 min Z4 with 90 sec easy. 30 min total threshold work.
5×4 min Z4 with 90 sec easy. 20 min total.

notes.about: APEX tempo — 30 min total threshold. The highest tempo volume of the cycle.
notes.week_context: Wk9 caps Z4 volume. Wk11 sharpens to 3×5 min Z4.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.advanced: Hold Z4 — no drift to Z5.

d5.title: Recovery Run
d5.stimulus: Active recovery. Deliberately easy. Don't compromise this — apex week needs the recovery dose.

notes.about: Recovery Run is critical at APEX — don't compromise the dose.
notes.week_context: Recovery Run holds 30 min through APEX.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d6.title: Compromised Running (apex)
d6.stimulus: Hardest Compromised session of the cycle. 6 rounds at race weight, except sandbag lunges programmed at half race distance (50m vs 100m) — see Framework Spec §9. Run target = goal race pace / high Z3, with Z4 ceiling only if mechanics stay clean (do not turn this into 6km of threshold). Includes Row as a Compromised exposure (closing the Row-under-fatigue practice gap).

structure.description: 6 rounds of (1 km run at goal race pace / high Z3, Z4 ceiling only if clean + 1 station at race weight). 90 sec rest between rounds.
details: Round 2: 1km + 50m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 1km + 50m sled pull at race weight
Round 4: 1km + 50m sandbag lunges at race weight (intentionally half race distance — see Framework Spec §9)
Round 1: 600m + 20 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 600m + 25m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 600m + 25m sled pull at race weight
Round 4: 600m + 37.5m sandbag lunges at race weight

notes.about: APEX Compromised. 6 rounds, race weight, with Row exposure added (closing v3.1 coverage gap). Sandbag lunges at half race distance per §9 to manage quad fatigue. Run target is goal race pace / high Z3 — don't turn it into 6km of threshold.
notes.week_context: Wk11 D5 sharpens this format down to 4 rounds at 800m at controlled effort.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: 5 rounds. Sled distances at 25m. Sandbag at 37.5m.
scaling.advanced: Hold per spec — Z4 only if mechanics stay clean.

notes.about: Peak Wk9 — APEX. Hardest week of the cycle. Maximum stimulus across strength, tempo, and Compromised. Full Rox strongly recommended for all sessions this week. Weekly running time peaks at ~150-190 min depending on athlete pace, typically landing ~160-180 min for most intermediate athletes.
notes.week_context: Wk9 caps cumulative stimulus. Wk10 reduces strength + adds Full Sim.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

weeks.title: Peak — Wk10 (Full Simulation)
weeks.summary: Full race simulation week. Volume reduced from Wk9 to allow recovery for the Sim. Sim runs at 80-85% race effort.

d1.title: Strength A (moderate, pre-Sim)
d1.stimulus: Pull back from Wk9 peak. RPE 7.

notes.about: Pull strength back to RPE 7 to allow recovery for Wk10 D5 Full Sim.
notes.week_context: Wk10 D1-D3 are pre-Sim moderation; D5 is the capstone Sim.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Easy Run pulls back to 40 min for pre-Sim recovery.
notes.week_context: Wk10 trim from Wk9 50 min peak.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d3.title: Strength B (moderate, pre-Sim)
d3.stimulus: Pull back from Wk9 apex. RPE 7.

structure.description: B1. Farmers Carry: 2×80m at 70% race weight

notes.about: Pre-Sim upper at RPE 7. Farmers reduce to 70% race weight for recovery margin.
notes.week_context: Wk10 D5 is the capstone Sim — pull back D1/D3 to support.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d4.title: Recovery Run (pre-Sim)
d4.stimulus: Active recovery. Deliberately easy. Last session before Full Sim.

notes.about: Pre-Sim Recovery Run trimmed to 25 min main work — reset before D5.
notes.week_context: Last session before Full Sim.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

d5.title: Full Simulation
d5.stimulus: Full race rehearsal at 80-85% race effort. Pacing, transitions, mental preparation. This is the capstone assessment of the cycle and the only session with no Quick Rox option. If you genuinely cannot do the Full Sim, defer to Wk8's Half-Sim format and treat this week as deferred rather than truncated.

structure.description: Full sim at race weight, RPE 8 (NOT race effort — 80-85%). Full duration estimate ~80-110 min depending on division and pace. Goal: smooth transitions, sustainable pace throughout. Capture splits and total time as benchmark.
details: 1 km run + Sled Push 50m at race weight
1 km run + Sled Pull 50m at race weight
1 km run + Farmers Carry 200m at race weight
1 km run + Sandbag Lunges 100m at race weight
1 km run + Wall Balls 100 reps at race weight

structure.description: No Quick Rox. Full Sim is the capstone assessment of the cycle. If you genuinely cannot do the Full Sim, defer to Wk8's Half-Sim format and treat this week as deferred rather than truncated.

notes.about: Capstone assessment of the cycle. All 8 stations + 8 runs at race weight, RPE 8 (not race effort — 80-85%). Capture splits and total time as benchmark for race day. No Quick Rox option — defer the week if you can't do the Full Sim.
notes.week_context: Wk10 D5 is the cycle capstone. Wk11 begins race-prep sharpening.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

scaling.beginner: Stay at 75-80% race effort. Sub Wk8 Half-Sim format if Full Sim is unrealistic.
scaling.advanced: Hold 80-85% — resist racing the Sim. Race effort is reserved for race day.

notes.substitutions: If a station is genuinely inaccessible, sub the closest equivalent (KB swings for sled push, DB carry for farmers, jumping squats for BBJ). Note: substitutions reduce the assessment value of this Sim.

notes.about: Peak Wk10 — Full Simulation. Strength + running pulled back from Wk9 to allow recovery for the capstone Sim. Sim runs at 80-85% race effort, all 8 stations, race weight throughout.
notes.week_context: Wk10 caps the Peak block. Wk11 begins race-prep sharpening.
notes.block_context: Peak block (Wk 7-10): highest stimulus block. Wk7 Mini-Deload allows Wk8-9-10 to deliver peak training stress. Wk8 Half-Sim covers race-order stations 5-8; Wk9 is APEX; Wk10 is Full Sim at 80-85%.

weeks.title: Race Prep — Wk11 (Sharpening)
weeks.summary: Sharpening. Meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained on key sessions. RPE cap 8.

d1.title: Strength A (sharpening)
d1.stimulus: Heavy but low volume — neural maintenance.

structure.description: A1. Back Squat: 4×3 @ RPE 8 (heavy but low volume — neural maintenance)

notes.about: Sharpening squat — 4×3 RPE 8. Low volume preserves race-week recovery.
notes.week_context: Wk11 keeps strength sharp; Wk12 D3 drops to RPE 5 for race-week maintenance.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

d2.title: Easy Run
d2.stimulus: Aerobic base. Conversational pace, nasal breathing if possible.

notes.about: Easy Run trims to 35 min for race-prep recovery.
notes.week_context: Wk11 35 min → Wk12 no Easy Run (race-week sessions are prescriptive).
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

d3.title: Strength B (sharpening)
d3.stimulus: Grip stays sharp at race weight. Volume reduced from Wk9 D3 to protect race-week recovery.

structure.description: B1. Farmers Carry: 2×80m at race weight (160m total at race weight; reduced from 2×100m to manage race-week recovery margin while preserving race-weight grip stimulus)

notes.about: Sharpening upper. Farmers at race weight, total volume cut 20% from Wk9 D3 (2×80m vs 2×100m) to preserve race-week recovery while keeping race-weight grip stimulus.
notes.week_context: Wk11 last race-weight farmers exposure before race day.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

d4.title: Tempo Run (threshold sharpening)
d4.stimulus: Main threshold sharpening exposure of race-prep week. Sharp without fatigue.

structure.description: 3×5 min Z4 with 2 min easy. 15 min total tempo, descending taper from Wk9's 30 min.
3×3 min Z4 with 2 min easy. 9 min total.

notes.about: Threshold sharpening — only Z4 exposure of the week. Half of Wk9's threshold volume.
notes.week_context: Wk11 D4 is the only Z4 session this week. D5 Compromised stays at high-Z3 cap.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

scaling.advanced: Hold Z4 — don't drift to Z5.

d5.title: Compromised Running (race rhythm, NOT threshold)
d5.stimulus: Race-pattern familiarity at controlled effort. Runs at goal race pace, capped at high Z3 / RPE 7-8 max — explicitly NOT threshold work. This is rhythm and movement-pattern practice, not a second threshold session. Lunge and sled distances at clean turf-marked sub-race volumes to protect race-week recovery.

structure.description: 4 rounds of (800m run at goal race pace, RPE 7-8 max + 1 station at race weight). 90 sec rest between rounds.
details: Round 2: 800m + 25m sled push at race weight (2 turf lengths)
Round 3: 800m + 37.5m sandbag lunges at race weight (3 turf lengths, reduced from race distance for race-week recovery)
Round 1: 500m + 15 wall balls at race weight
Round 2: 500m + 12.5m sled push at race weight
Round 3: 500m + 25m sandbag lunges at race weight

notes.about: Race-rhythm Compromised. NOT a second threshold session. Runs at goal race pace, RPE 7-8 max — explicit Z3 ceiling. Sled and sandbag distances at sub-race volumes for race-week recovery.
notes.week_context: Last Compromised exposure before race day. Wk12 has no Compromised.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

scaling.advanced: Hold per spec — race rhythm, not threshold.

notes.about: Race Prep Wk11 — sharpening. Meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained on key sessions. D4 is the only Z4 exposure this week; D5 is race-rhythm Compromised at controlled effort (high-Z3 cap).
notes.week_context: Wk11 sharpens; Wk12 is final taper.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

weeks.title: Race Prep — Wk12 (Race Week)
weeks.summary: Race week. Final sharp taper. Race -N labels are race-relative. Set race date in Settings.

d1.title: Race -6 — Continuous Light EMOM
d1.stimulus: Movement maintenance at light load. Practice race-pace mechanics without fatigue. NOT a simulation — a movement-maintenance EMOM at sub-50% race weight.

structure.description: 20 min EMOM rotation (5 full rounds), all at LIGHT load (~50-60% race weight).
16 min EMOM (4 full rounds) with same rotation, same rep counts.

notes.about: Race -6 (six days before race day). Light EMOM for movement maintenance — not a simulation. Sub-50% race weight; light loads only.
notes.week_context: Wk12 sequence: Race -6 EMOM → Race -4 Tempo touches → Race -3 light Strength → Race -2 shake-out → Race day.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

scaling.advanced: Hold light loads — race readiness over fatigue.

notes.substitutions: KB swings (sled push), DB lunges (sandbag), jumping squats (BBJ).

d2.title: Race -4 — Tempo Run (race-pace touches)
d2.stimulus: Brief race-pace exposure to keep tempo sharp without taxing recovery.

structure.description: 3×3 min at goal race pace with 2 min easy between.
3×2 min at goal race pace with 2 min easy.

notes.about: Race -4 (four days before race day). Brief race-pace exposure. 9 min total at goal race pace — sharp but minimal stimulus.
notes.week_context: Last running intensity before race day.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

scaling.advanced: Hold goal race pace exactly — don't push faster.

d3.title: Race -3 — Strength (full body, very light)
d3.stimulus: Movement maintenance only. Wake the nervous system without depleting it.

structure.description: A1. Squat (any variation): 3×5 @ RPE 5 (very light, 4-5 reps in reserve)

notes.about: Race -3 (three days before race day). Movement maintenance — wake the nervous system without depleting it. RPE 5 = 4-5 reps in reserve. Quick Rox = Full Rox (already minimal).
notes.week_context: Last strength session before race day.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).
notes.substitutions: Any squat / press / row variant — pick what's lowest-friction to set up.

d4.title: Race -2 — Recovery Run (shake-out)
d4.stimulus: Easy shake-out 1-2 days before race. Loosen up, no stimulus accumulation.

notes.about: Race -2 (two days before race day). Easy shake-out + brief strides at race pace. Loosen up; no stimulus accumulation.
notes.week_context: Final session before race day. Race day is out of scope for v3.2.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).

scaling.advanced: Hold 4×30 sec strides at race pace exactly — don't extend.

notes.about: Race Prep Wk12 — race week. Final sharp taper with very low total volume. Sessions use Race -N labels (Race -6, Race -4, Race -3, Race -2). The app prompts for race date and calculates calendar placement; non-racers can run the four sessions sequentially with rest days between, ignoring the labels. Race day is out of scope for v3.2.
notes.week_context: Wk12 is the final week. Race day is Race 0 — out of scope. Post-race recovery handled separately.
notes.block_context: Race Prep block (Wk 11-12): declining sessions. Wk11 sharpening (meaningful volume reduction with intensity maintained). Wk12 race week (final sharp taper, no full or partial multi-station simulations within final 7 days, race day fresh).
